content: We can reframe Self-Refine's iterative self-feedback loop as a base framework that can be augmented by multiple inconsistent reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to solve the problem of single-perspective blind spots in current no-training self-refinement. This hybrid approach would retain Self-Refine's key advantage of requiring no supervised data or additional training while fixing the common failure mode where a single LLM cannot recognize its own logical or structural errors.
